name: Endeavour Silver Corp.

profile.description: Endeavour Silver Corp. is a mid-tier silver mining company operating three producing mines: Guanacevi in Durango, Mexico; Terronera in Jalisco, Mexico (commercial production commenced late 2025); and Kolpa in Peru (acquired May 2025). The company also holds the large-scale Pitarrilla silver development project. Q1 2026 revenue surged 230% to $209.7M driven by Terronera and Kolpa ramp-ups and higher silver prices. Headquartered in Vancouver, Canada, incorporated in 1981.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Endeavour executed a transformational portfolio overhaul in 2025-2026 — building the Terronera mine from greenfield to commercial production, acquiring the Kolpa mine in Peru expanding into base metals (lead, zinc, copper), and divesting the legacy Bolanitos mine for $55.9M in January 2026 [1][2]. This multi-pronged repositioning from a two-mine Mexico-only operator to a diversified three-mine, two-country producer represents genuine business model transformation. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI products or quantified AI-driven operational savings reported; mining operations remain conventional [1]. Market Share (1/2): Silver equivalent production of 3.34M oz in Q1 2026 (+78% YoY) positions Endeavour as a meaningful mid-tier producer, with 2026 guidance of 14.6-15.6M AgEq oz establishing it among the top 10 primary silver miners globally [1]. Product Pipeline (2/2): Pitarrilla development project ($65.8M capital allocated in 2026), Kolpa expansion into polymetals, and Terronera grade improvement through higher-grade zones in H2 2026 provide concrete next-generation production visibility [1][2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Mining companies are below-average R&D spenders as a sector.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Endeavour's purpose centers on responsibly growing silver production to meet demand from the energy transition — relevant given silver's critical role in solar panels and electronics — but functional rather than distinctive or deeply articulated beyond industry norms [3]. Community Impact (1/2): The company maintains community partnership programs across Mexico and Peru operations, including social license investment at Terronera and Kolpa communities, and has reported on ESG initiatives for ten years [3]. No specific dollar amounts for community investment cited. Employee Retention (1/2): Glassdoor reviews rate Endeavour 4.1 for culture/values and 4.4 for senior management, with employees describing a 'wonderful corporate culture that is very fair and socially responsible' [4]. The ICARE Values program formalizes 'The Endeavour Way.' However, specific retention or tenure data is unavailable. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful discretionary insider purchases by CEO Dan Dickson or other executives in the last 12 months.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. D/E of 39.0% reflects conservative leverage with $231.8M cash and $173.4M working capital against $236.1M in convertible notes due 2031 [1]. ROE is -3.9% trailing due to FY2025 net loss of $119.1M (dominated by $126.2M derivative losses), though Q1 2026 already generated $64.9M net income suggesting a sharp inflection [1][2]. Revenue growth YoY of 230% reflects the transformational addition of Terronera and Kolpa, and the 3-year revenue CAGR of 30.5% from $210.2M (2022) to $467.5M (2025) demonstrates sustained trajectory [1]. Forward P/E of 7.2x is deeply attractive against the 20x target. Profit margin is -3.5% trailing, but Q1 2026 showed 31% net margin ($64.9M/$209.7M) as operations normalize [2]. Forward PEG excluded (unavailable). Weighted sum: D/E(1x1.0) + ROE(2x0.0) + RevYoY(1x1.0) + Rev5yrCAGR(2x1.0) + FwdPE(1x1.0) + ProfitMargin(1x0.0) = 5.0/8 = 6.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Silver mining is a physically intensive, capital-heavy activity involving underground ore extraction, mineral processing, and metal refining — AI cannot substitute for any of this core value chain [1]. Endeavour operates mines in remote locations in Mexico and Peru with complex geological, regulatory, and community-relations requirements that are structurally beyond AI's reach. More critically, AI is a demand driver for silver: AI data centers require massive electrical infrastructure using silver-bearing solar panels, and silver is an essential component in electronics and connectors used in AI hardware [5]. The 67M oz projected 2026 global silver market deficit is partly driven by AI-adjacent demand. Defense against disruption is near-maximum for a physical commodity producer. AI Opportunity (1/1): While Endeavour does not sell AI products, silver's role as a critical input material for AI infrastructure (solar panels powering data centers, electronics, thermal conductors) means the company directly benefits from AI-driven demand growth. Industry analysts project silver demand from electronics and solar to grow 8-12% annually through 2030, partly driven by AI energy requirements [5].

infinite_game.summary: Endeavour Silver demonstrates meaningful long-term investment discipline — building Terronera from greenfield, acquiring Kolpa to diversify geographically, and developing the large-scale Pitarrilla project all reflect multi-decade thinking. CEO Dan Dickson's portfolio rationalization (divesting Bolanitos, entering Peru) shows strategic intentionality. However, the $350M convertible note issuance and aggressive growth trajectory create financial leverage that could constrain flexibility if silver prices decline, and the company's competitive language is growth-oriented rather than purpose-driven.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Endeavour's purpose around responsible silver production for the energy transition is relevant but not deeply distinctive — most silver miners could articulate a similar cause [3]. The company has been mining silver since 2004 with consistent operational focus, but the purpose has not been tested through existential crisis in a way that validates it as a true Just Cause. The ICARE Values program is a positive cultural initiative but is more operational than aspirational.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Building Terronera from greenfield during a period of silver price uncertainty required significant capital commitment ($200M+) before first production revenue — a meaningful long-term bet [1]. The simultaneous Kolpa acquisition and Bolanitos divestiture show willingness to reshape the portfolio. However, the $350M convertible note issuance in December 2025 at 0.25% interest, while well-timed, adds leverage that increases financial risk [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Glassdoor ratings of 4.1 for culture/values and 4.4 for senior management are above average for the mining sector [4]. The ICARE Values program and ISO 14001/9001 certifications suggest organizational discipline. CEO Dickson has maintained consistent leadership through the Terronera construction and Kolpa integration. However, with 2,160 employees across three countries, trust varies by operation, and the Terronera security stoppage in early 2026 suggests external challenges to team stability.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Divesting legacy Bolanitos while acquiring Kolpa in Peru and commissioning Terronera fundamentally repositioned Endeavour from a two-mine Mexico operator to a diversified three-mine, two-country producer with polymetallic capability [1][2]. This is meaningful strategic evolution, though it was incremental and driven by standard mining portfolio optimization rather than a transformative reimagining of the business model.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Endeavour's competitive language focuses on production growth, cost reduction, and pipeline expansion rather than acknowledging what First Majestic, Pan American Silver, or MAG Silver do better [1]. Management commentary is growth-oriented ('transformational quarter,' 'record production') rather than learning-oriented. No evidence of using rivals' strengths for self-improvement.

investment_thesis: Endeavour Silver delivered a transformational Q1 2026 with $209.7M revenue (+230% YoY), $64.9M net income (vs. $32.9M loss), and silver equivalent production of 3.34M oz (+78% YoY), driven by contributions from the newly commissioned Terronera mine ($84.2M revenue) and acquired Kolpa operation ($55.8M revenue) [1][2]. The company enters 2026 guidance of 14.6-15.6M AgEq oz at $27-28/oz AISC with $231.8M cash and $173.4M working capital, positioning it for material free cash flow generation if silver prices sustain above $30/oz [1]. Forward P/E of 7.2x against $1.32 forward EPS implies the market has not fully priced the production step-change, and the large-scale Pitarrilla development project provides a multi-decade growth optionality that is currently valued at near-zero.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on execution risk and cost inflation. Q1 2026 AISC of $37.03/oz (+51% YoY) significantly exceeded the $27-28/oz full-year guidance, requiring substantial cost improvement in subsequent quarters through grade optimization and throughput scaling [2]. The $126.2M derivative loss in FY2025 (gold swaps and silver collars) demonstrates the hazard of hedging in a rising metals environment, and $24.2M in additional derivative losses in Q1 2026 suggest this drag continues [2]. The $350M convertible note issuance at 0.25% adds leverage that amplifies downside risk if silver falls below $28/oz. Mexico security risks (Terronera stoppage in February 2026) and Peru political uncertainty (Kolpa permitting) create operational tail risks. The trailing ROE of -3.9% and profit margin of -3.5% reflect a company that has not yet proven sustainable profitability at scale.
key_catalysts: Terronera grade improvement in H2 2026 as the mine accesses higher-grade zones, expected to drive AISC from $37/oz toward the $27-28/oz guidance range and unlock material free cash flow
Silver price sustained above $35/oz benefiting from AI data center power demand (solar panels), electronics growth, and a projected 67M oz global supply deficit in 2026
Pitarrilla development advancement — if Endeavour publishes a preliminary economic assessment or begins permitting, it would add multi-decade production visibility to the portfolio
key_risks: Q1 2026 AISC of $37.03/oz is 33% above the $27-28/oz full-year guidance, and if grade improvements at Terronera do not materialize as expected, free cash flow will remain constrained despite record production

Mexico security and political risk remains elevated — the February 2026 Terronera security stoppage, combined with resource nationalism trends in Mexico's mining legislation, could disrupt operations at two of three mines
The $350M convertible note and $44.2M copper stream liability add significant financial leverage that amplifies downside exposure if silver declines below $28-30/oz breakeven levels
